Understanding Cross-Border Fees from Australia to Australia

When processing international payments between Australia and Australia, businesses often overlook hidden costs. Both Stripe and PayPal charge a base domestic fee, but international transactions trigger additional surcharges.

The Hidden Cost of Cross-Border Payments

If your business is located in Australia and you charge a customer's card issued in Australia, you typically face an International Card Surcharge. Furthermore, if the transaction requires currency conversion, a Currency Conversion Surcharge is applied. Our calculator breaks down these exact percentages so you know your true net profit.

Stripe vs. PayPal for Global B2B

While Stripe often separates the international card fee and currency conversion fee, PayPal often bundles them into a higher flat percentage or applies differing fixed fees based on the specific corridor. Always compare the Net Received amount to determine the most cost-effective gateway for your Australia customers.

Disclaimer: These are estimates based on standard public B2B pricing. Your actual rates may vary depending on enterprise agreements, specific card types, and exact live currency exchange rates.
